title: Mahalasa Narayani Temple
tagline: Mahalasa Narayani Temple — spiritual town of Goa

overview.short: Mahalasa Narayani Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Mohini as Mahalasa, located in Mardol, Ponda, in the Indian state of Goa.
overview.description: Mahalasa Narayani Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the goddess Mohini as Mahalasa, located in Mardol, Ponda, in the Indian state of Goa.

topPlaces.name: Mangueshi
topPlaces.description: Mangueshi is a village located within Ponda taluka in the coastal state of Goa, India. Mangueshi or Mangueshim are other variations for the same name.

topPlaces.name: Velinga
topPlaces.description: Velinga is a village in the North Goa sub-district (or taluka) of Ponda. It lies in the centre of Goa, in a setting amidst greenery and temples.

topPlaces.name: Velinga (Veling)
topPlaces.description: Velinga or Veling is a village in Ponda taluka, Goa. The panchayat (village council) area includes well-known temples, such as the Lord Manguesh Temple.

topPlaces.name: IIT Goa
topPlaces.description: Indian Institute of Technology Goa (IIT Goa or IITGoa) is an autonomous public university located in Goa and an institute of National Importance. Ever since an IIT was allotted to Goa by the Central government in 2014, the new Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) at Goa started functioning from July, 2016 in a temporary campus housed at Goa Engineering College (GEC).

topPlaces.name: Cuncoliem
topPlaces.description: Cuncoliem is a village in Ponda taluka, Goa, India.

topPlaces.name: Mardol, Goa
topPlaces.description: Mardol is a census town in Ponda taluka, North Goa district in the state of Goa, India. The Marathi and Portuguese languages radio playwright and short-story writer Ananta Rau Sar Dessai lived and practiced medicine in Mardol.

topPlaces.name: Priol
topPlaces.description: Priol is a village in Goa, India. It is situated in the Ponda taluka.

topPlaces.name: Farmagudi
topPlaces.description: Farmagudi is a suburb of Ponda town in Goa, India. It falls under the jurisdiction of the Ponda taluka.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mahalasa Narayani Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mahalasa Narayani Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mahalasa Narayani Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (17 km) and the nearest railway station is Vasco da Gama (Goa) (18 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mahalasa Narayani Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mahalasa Narayani Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mahalasa Narayani Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Mahalasa Narayani Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mahalasa Narayani Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mangueshi, Velinga, Velinga (Veling), IIT Goa, Cuncoliem.
